description: Microsoft Remote Procedure Call — RPC endpoint enumeration, remote command execution via WMI/DCOM/Task Scheduler, and lateral movement.

tools.name: rpcclient
tools.description: Samba RPC client for interactive enumeration of domain users, groups, and policies via named pipes.

commands.title: Null session — connect anonymously
commands.description: Connect with a null session (no credentials). Run enumeration commands interactively.

commands.title: Authenticated session
commands.description: Connect with valid domain credentials.

commands.title: Enumerate domain users
commands.description: List all domain users with their RIDs.

commands.title: Enumerate domain groups
commands.description: List all domain groups with their RIDs.

commands.title: Query specific user by RID
commands.description: Get detailed info for a user by their RID (e.g., 500 for Administrator).

commands.title: Lookup username to SID
commands.description: Resolve a username to its SID.

commands.title: Lookup SID to username
commands.description: Resolve a SID to its username.

commands.title: Display user info (querydispinfo)
commands.description: Display extended info for all users (name, description, flags).

commands.title: Enumerate group members
commands.description: List RIDs of all members in a group (e.g., 512 for Domain Admins).

commands.title: Get domain password policy
commands.description: Retrieve the domain password policy (min length, complexity, etc.).

commands.title: Enumerate printers
commands.description: Enumerate shared printers (useful for PrintNightmare / SpoolSample attacks).

commands.title: Enumerate privileges
commands.description: List all available privileges on the target.

commands.title: Get domain SID
commands.description: Get the domain name and SID via LSA query.

commands.title: Enumerate trusted domains
commands.description: Enumerate SIDs known to the LSA (reveals trust relationships).

commands.title: Null session — enumerate users
commands.description: Attempt to enumerate domain users with null session.

commands.title: Null session — get password policy
commands.description: Attempt to get password policy via null session.

tools.name: rpcdump.py (Impacket)
tools.description: Dump all registered RPC endpoints on the target to discover services and interfaces.

commands.title: Dump RPC endpoints (unauthenticated)
commands.description: List all RPC endpoints without authentication.

commands.title: Dump RPC endpoints (authenticated)
commands.description: List all RPC endpoints with valid credentials.

commands.title: Dump endpoints on specific port
commands.description: Dump RPC endpoints specifically from the endpoint mapper on port 135.

tools.name: wmiexec.py (Impacket)
tools.description: Semi-interactive shell over WMI (DCOM port 135). Does not drop a binary on disk. One of the stealthiest Impacket execution methods.

commands.title: Shell with password
commands.description: Get a semi-interactive shell via WMI using password authentication.

commands.title: Shell with NTLM hash (PtH)
commands.description: Get a shell via WMI using pass-the-hash.

commands.title: Execute single command
commands.description: Execute a single command via WMI without dropping into a shell.

commands.title: Execute command with PtH
commands.description: Execute a single command via WMI using pass-the-hash.

commands.title: Shell with Kerberos auth
commands.description: Get a shell via WMI using Kerberos authentication (requires valid ccache).

commands.title: Shell as local admin
commands.description: Get a shell using a local administrator account (note the './' prefix).

commands.title: Execute PowerShell command
commands.description: Execute an encoded PowerShell command via WMI.

tools.name: dcomexec.py (Impacket)
tools.description: Semi-interactive shell via DCOM objects (MMC20.Application, ShellWindows, ShellBrowserWindow). Does not create a service or drop binaries.

commands.title: Shell via MMC20 with password
commands.description: Get a shell using the default MMC20.Application DCOM object.

commands.title: Shell via MMC20 with PtH
commands.description: Get a shell via MMC20.Application using pass-the-hash.

commands.title: Shell via ShellWindows object
commands.description: Get a shell using the ShellWindows DCOM object.

commands.title: Shell via ShellBrowserWindow object
commands.description: Get a shell using the ShellBrowserWindow DCOM object.

commands.title: Execute single command via MMC20
commands.description: Execute a single command via DCOM without an interactive shell.

commands.title: Shell via MMC20 with Kerberos
commands.description: Get a shell via DCOM using Kerberos authentication.

commands.title: Shell via ShellWindows with PtH
commands.description: Get a shell via ShellWindows using pass-the-hash.

tools.name: atexec.py (Impacket)
tools.description: Execute commands via the Windows Task Scheduler service (ATSVC). Creates a scheduled task, runs the command, and retrieves output.

commands.title: Execute command with password
commands.description: Execute a command via Task Scheduler using password authentication.

commands.title: Execute command with PtH
commands.description: Execute a command via Task Scheduler using pass-the-hash.

commands.title: Execute command with Kerberos
commands.description: Execute a command via Task Scheduler using Kerberos authentication.

commands.title: Dump system info
commands.description: Run systeminfo via scheduled task to enumerate OS details.

commands.title: Read a file via Task Scheduler
commands.description: Read a remote file by executing 'type' via scheduled task.

commands.title: Execute PowerShell payload
commands.description: Execute an encoded PowerShell command via Task Scheduler.

tools.name: lookupsid.py (Impacket)
tools.description: SID brute-forcing tool to enumerate domain users and groups via RPC.

commands.title: Brute-force SIDs with credentials
commands.description: Enumerate users and groups by brute-forcing RIDs via LSA lookups.

commands.title: Brute-force SIDs with null session
commands.description: Enumerate users and groups via guest or null session.

commands.title: Brute-force SIDs with specific range
commands.description: Enumerate users and groups up to RID 10000.

commands.title: Brute-force SIDs with PtH
commands.description: Enumerate users and groups using pass-the-hash.
